---
name: Product or owner decision
about: Resolve a choice that requires human product judgment or owner authority
title: "Decision: "
labels: "decision, human-required"
assignees: ""
---

## Decision needed

State the choice that requires product judgment or owner authority.

## Context

Explain why the decision matters, what already exists, and which work it
affects.

## Options

### Option A

Describe benefits, costs, constraints, and reversibility.

### Option B

Describe benefits, costs, constraints, and reversibility.

## Decision criteria

- Product value and user impact
- Privacy, safety, trust, and operational consequences
- Compatibility with current architecture and documented scope
- Cost, maintenance, and reversibility
- Evidence available now and uncertainty that remains

## Non-goals

- Implementation not authorized by this decision issue
- Adjacent product choices owned elsewhere

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Viable options and consequences are explicit.
- [ ] Relevant technical research and repository behavior are summarized.
- [ ] The owner records a selected option and rationale.
- [ ] Durable architecture or policy is promoted to an ADR when warranted.
- [ ] Focused implementation and documentation follow-ups are created.
- [ ] Parent tracking issues are updated.

## Agent assistance

An explicitly assigned agent may perform read-only research, compare options,
and prepare drafts. It may not substitute its preference for the required human
decision or ship product behavior from this issue.

## Result

Record the selected option, rationale, consequences, open risks, and follow-up
issues.
